# Layered Reasoning Framework (LRF) — General Audience Overview (v0.1)

## The Problem with AI Today

You've probably used ChatGPT, or Siri, or one of the AI tools that keeps popping up everywhere. And you've probably noticed that sometimes it's incredibly helpful, and sometimes it just misses. It rambles, it ignores what you actually asked for, or it gives you something completely different from what you expected.

## Why That Happens

That inconsistency isn't a bug exactly. It's just how these tools work by default. They're powerful, but they don't have structure unless you give it to them. Most people don't know how to do that, so they get frustrating results and assume that's just how AI is.

## What I Built

I built something that changes that. It's called the Layered Reasoning Framework (LRF). In simple terms, it's a system that tells any AI tool how to think through a task before it answers. It gives the AI a clear goal, a defined approach, and a set of rules to follow. The result: the AI becomes consistent, focused, and actually does what you ask it to do.

## The Memory Card

The other piece I built is called the Memory Card. Imagine you've spent real time learning how to use an AI tool, teaching it who you are, how you think, and what you need. Then you switch to a different one and all of that is just gone. The Memory Card solves that. It carries everything over so you're never starting from scratch.

## Why It Matters

Together these two things make AI feel like something you can actually rely on.
